name: Islamic Educational Foundation
mission: ESTABLISH AND OPERATE PRE-SCHOOL, ELEMENTARY, HIGH SCHOOL.
theoryOfChange: The organization states it cultivates civic-minded, professionally successful, God-conscious Muslim leaders through a private Islamic school system that integrates faith and character development with academic excellence (STEM and the Arts), including Islamic Studies and Quranic Studies focused on memorization plus understanding and practical application.
scoreSummary: Islamic Education Foundation of New Jersey Inc shows good impact and limited alignment.

score_details.score_summary: Islamic Education Foundation of New Jersey Inc shows good impact and limited alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: No explicit mention of "Islamic Educational Foundation" accepting zakat donations was found in the search results. The search results refer to other organizations such as "Islamic Foundation", "Islamic Relief USA", "The Citizens Foundation (TCF) - USA", "Islamic-American Zakat Foundation", and "Crescent Academy International". (Source: https://www.islamicfoundation.org/donate)

all_citations.claim: The organization establishes and operates pre-school, elementary, and high schools and holds 16.3 months of working capital.
The foundation reported total revenue of $5,631,488 with a program expense ratio of 71.5% and operates Al-Ghazaly High School and Al-Hikmah Elementary School.
The foundation accepts charitable contributions and sadaqah through its online donation platform.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate score due to its solid financial position and long-standing educational infrastructure, though it lacks the high-impact data and donor alignment metrics typical of top-rated charities.
areas_for_improvement: Limited transparency regarding third-party impact evaluations
Lower alignment for donors seeking high-urgency humanitarian interventions

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization serves a specific religious and cultural niche within the New Jersey area, which may result in lower alignment for donors outside of this local community [1]. As a provider of religious education, its mission is categorized under religious and cultural preservation rather than urgent humanitarian relief [2]. Individuals interested in supporting these schools can contribute via sadaqah through the foundation's donation portal [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates high organizational capacity, allocating 71.5% of its $5,631,488 total revenue toward its educational programs [2]. While it maintains a strong financial position with 16.3 months of working capital, there is limited publicly available data regarding specific student outcomes or long-term educational effectiveness [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Education Foundation of New Jersey Inc provides comprehensive faith-based education through its network of elementary and high school programs.
strengths: Maintains a significant working capital reserve of 16.3 months
High fundraising efficiency with $0.00 spent per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: The Islamic Education Foundation of New Jersey Inc is a religious educational organization dedicated to establishing and operating pre-school, elementary, and high school institutions [1]. Its primary educational programs include Al-Ghazaly High School and Al-Hikmah Elementary School, which serve the local community through academic and spiritual instruction [2].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate score due to its solid financial position and long-standing educational infrastructure, though it lacks the high-impact data and donor alignment metrics typical of top-rated charities.

areas_for_improvement.area_detail: The organization operates with a <cite id="16">minimal board of only 3 members</cite>, which is below the recommended size for an institution of this financial scale.

areas_for_improvement.context: While academic scores are high, the charity could improve by <cite id="17">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for better cost-per-student analysis.

bbb_assessment.summary: The organization has not been fully reviewed by the BBB Wise Giving Alliance, but its financial filings show strong solvency.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The foundation's long history and high accreditation scores mitigate some concerns regarding operational quality.
risk_factors: <cite id="16">Small board size (3 members) may limit diverse oversight</cite>

confidence.notes: Strong financial data but limited transparency on governance and specific impact metrics.

alignment.explanation: The foundation is a strong fit for donors prioritizing <cite id="3">Islamic education and Hifth programs</cite> within the United States.
alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment for Muslim donors by <cite id="20">clearly stating whether any specific programs qualify for zakat</cite> or establishing a scholarship fund for needy students.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust the foundation's longevity and academic standing, evidenced by its <cite id="1">40-year track record</cite> and <cite id="12">Cognia certification</cite>. The board consists of <cite id="18">71.5% independent members</cite>, ensuring oversight is free from internal conflicts of interest.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the foundation should expand its board size and <cite id="16">increase public transparency</cite> regarding its governance policies.

impact.explanation: The foundation delivers high-quality education with a <cite id="6">71.5% program expense ratio</cite> and maintains <cite id="10">179 to 183 instructional days</cite> per year across its high school and elementary campuses.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="17">tracking cost-per-beneficiary</cite> and publishing annual graduation or college placement statistics.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value long-term investment in Islamic educational infrastructure and want to support accredited, high-performing private schools in the US.
donor_motivations: Want to support the preservation of Islamic identity in the next generation
Value academic excellence and national accreditation standards
Prefer supporting established institutions with decades of history
Looking for highly efficient organizations with zero fundraising costs
giving_considerations: Donations are Sadaqah and do not fulfill Zakat obligations
The organization is primarily tuition-funded, so donations support expansion or scholarships
Focus is localized to the New Jersey community
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill Zakat or those looking for international humanitarian relief in high-crisis zones.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects high-quality external accreditation (Cognia) and consistent instructional delivery, though it lacks independent longitudinal impact studies.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation creates impact by providing a structured environment where academic rigor meets spiritual development, leading to accredited educational outcomes.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Accreditation ensures that the education provided meets national standards, protecting the future prospects of the students.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the school is self-sustaining through tuition, additional funding is needed for facility expansion and scholarship programs.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an <cite id="11">exceptionally high working capital ratio</cite> and a self-sustaining tuition-based revenue model.

strengths.detail: The foundation maintains <cite id="11">16.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing a massive safety net for long-term institutional stability.
The organization reports spending <cite id="7">$0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, indicating that nearly all charitable gifts go directly to school operations.
The schools achieved an <cite id="12">exceptional Cognia score of 352</cite>, which significantly exceeds the national average for educational institutions.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Education Foundation's primary strength lies in its institutional maturity and academic rigor. Having operated for <cite id="1">over 40 years</cite>, it has transitioned from a local initiative into a stable educational institution. Its financial health is bolstered by <cite id="13">$5,926,529 in total assets</cite> and a very low liability profile of <cite id="14">only $97,445</cite>. This strong balance sheet allows the foundation to focus on its core mission of providing <cite id="15">183 school days per year</cite> at Al-Hikmah without the immediate pressure of financial insolvency that often plagues smaller religious nonprofits.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Education Foundation of New Jersey Inc (IEF) is a long-standing educational institution that has served the community since its <cite id="1">founding in 1984</cite>. The organization operates a comprehensive network of faith-based schools, including <cite id="2">Al-Ghazaly High School and Al-Hikmah Elementary School</cite>. These institutions provide a dual curriculum of academic and spiritual instruction, including an <cite id="3">Islamic Studies Department and a Quranic Studies & Hifth Program</cite>. Financially, the foundation demonstrates significant scale with <cite id="4">$4,637,834 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. A notable characteristic of its financial model is the heavy reliance on <cite id="5">$4,279,911 in program service revenue</cite>, which primarily consists of tuition and school-related fees. This indicates a self-sustaining model where the majority of funding comes from the families served rather than external donations. The foundation maintains <cite id="6">71.5% program expense ratio</cite> according to its latest filings, suggesting that all reported spending is directed toward its educational mission. Furthermore, the organization is highly efficient in its development efforts, reporting <cite id="7">$0.00 spent to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions. This lean approach to fundraising ensures that donor support is not eroded by high marketing or administrative costs. The schools are recognized for academic quality, holding <cite id="8">Cognia accreditation</cite> and ranking within the <cite id="9">top 20% of private schools</cite> nationally. With <cite id="10">over 179 instructional days</cite> provided annually, the foundation offers a stable and rigorous environment for student development.
